nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: l'Isle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northumberland, Durham, and Yorkshire): variant of   Lyle   and a cognate of 2 below.
French: habitational name from L'Isle or Lisle, names of several places in various parts of France, named with Old French isle ‘island’ (from Latin insula). Compare Delisle .

variants.name: Lyle
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin): topographic or habitational name from Anglo-Norman French del isle ‘from the island’ (from Latin de insula), denoting someone who lived on an island or who came from a place in France named Ile, Isle, or L'Isle. The surname may also in some cases be from Lille (Nord). Compare Lisle and Iles .

variants.name: Lile
meanings: English and French: variant of   Lisle  .

nameMeanings.name: Humphrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Humphrey de l'Isle was born in 1032, in Castle Combe, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert de L'Isle, was 33 and his mother, Rohese de Wahall, was 20. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1086, in his hometown, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Humphrey de l'Isle est né en 1032, à Castle Combe, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de L'Isle, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Rohese de Wahall, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1086, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Humphrey de l'Isle(이)가 1032년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 카스틀 코브에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de L'Isle(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Rohese de Wahall(은)는 20세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1086년 그의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.
